name: Co-packaged optics (CPO)
severityNote: Real but timing uncertain; pluggables dominate near-term
description: Co-packaged optics integrate optical engines onto the switch/GPU substrate rather than into a pluggable cage, slashing electrical-channel loss and pluggable-transceiver power overhead. NVIDIA Quantum-X (CPO switch) and Broadcom Bailly are the leading commercial efforts; volume ramp is gated by yield, optical-engine modularity, and serviceability — not by underlying technology readiness.
stackPosition: InP wafer (or III-V-on-Si) → laser die → optical engine (Lightwave Logic / Ayar / NVIDIA in-house) → ELS (external laser source) module → switch/GPU substrate co-package integration → 2.5D/3D packaging at TSMC → board / rack. CPO compresses 3-4 layers of the optical stack into the package step, with most value migrating upstream to laser-source vendors and integrators.

supplyStructure.name: Coherent / Lumentum (laser die supply)
supplyStructure.note: Underlying laser supply for ELS modules

indicators.name: NVIDIA Quantum-X CPO switch volume shipment milestones
Broadcom Bailly CPO product disclosures + hyperscaler design wins
TSMC advanced packaging commentary mentioning CPO-coupling
OFC and ECOC CPO panel + commercial demo announcements
Ayar Labs commercial design wins + funding events

companies.name: TSMC
companies.note: Substrate-side integrator; captures packaging margin on each CPO die

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.note: Quantum-X CPO is the lead reference design; in-house optical engine

companies.name: Coherent / Lumentum
companies.note: Underlying laser supply regardless of CPO vs pluggable mix

companies.name: Ayar Labs
companies.note: TeraPHY + ELS architecture; lead-design partner with NVIDIA and Intel for CPO
companies.investors: NVIDIA, AMD, Intel Capital, HPE, GlobalFoundries

companies.name: Lightmatter
companies.note: Passage photonic interconnect substrate — direct alternative to CPO at the package level

companies.name: Celestial AI
companies.note: Photonic Fabric — competes with both CPO and HBM-side high-bandwidth approaches
companies.investors: US Innovative Technology Fund, Temasek, Porsche

companies.name: NcodiN
companies.note: III-V-on-Si monolithic nanolaser approach to ELS

insight: CPO is real technology with uncertain volume timing — the industry has been called 'CPO next year' for five consecutive years. The investable framing is that pluggable transceivers remain dominant through 2027, with CPO ramping as a complement at the highest bandwidth tiers rather than a replacement. The asymmetric long is laser-source vendors (Coherent, Lumentum) whose demand is mix-agnostic. The widow-maker is calling CPO too early and shorting transceiver makers — that trade has burned multiple managers in 2024-2025. Watch ELS architecture wins as the leading indicator: if Ayar Labs or NcodiN closes a hyperscaler GPU deal, the curve steepens fast.

debates.question: Does CPO exceed 10% of new fabric port shipments by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Likely below — pluggable inertia plus serviceability concerns drag the ramp

debates.question: Does a non-NVIDIA hyperscaler adopt CPO at volume by 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Possible Google / Meta tertiary deployments; volume adoption unlikely
